scoring_note: YES -- a mild severity inversion recurs tonight and is being reported, not silently corrected. Applying priority_score = news_score + base_priority literally, single-day/single-incident items -- Algiers (73), Gabon (67), Azerbaijan (67), Delhi (63), Ibadan (63), Verizon (60), Tucson (60) -- rank at or above ongoing mass-casualty/mass-atrocity situations -- Ukraine (57), Russia (57), Iran (57), Sudan (57), Democratic Republic of C (57), Israel (57), Armenia (57), Palestine (57) -- which are tied at 57. The mechanism: every one of these countries was reassessed within the last 7-18 days (staleness_score=0, identical to the single-incident entities), so the tie-breaker becomes volatility_score (a +10 boundary-proximity bonus several single-incident entities also carry) and importance_score (Fortune-500=15 vs countries=12, cities=8). This is materially less severe than recent nights Fortune-500-dominates-countries pattern -- no evidenced Fortune-500 or university entity this cycle outranks an active-conflict country -- but a human should confirm that a one-off orphanage fire, opposition-lawyer statement, or border skirmish outranking an ongoing siege/genocide-marker conflict is the intended behavior of the priority formula before the assessor treats top_entities as a strict priority queue.

top_entities.name: Algiers
top_entities.news_summary: Fire at an orphanage on the outskirts of Algiers killed 11 people (including children) and injured 19 (Jul 16), amid a heatwave that had prompted 913 fire responses nationwide since Jul 8.

top_entities.name: Azerbaijan
top_entities.news_summary: Outbreak of border violence between Azerbaijan and Armenia (Jul 12-16), including exchanges of heavy-weapon fire and drone use.

top_entities.name: Gabon
top_entities.news_summary: Lawyers for Gabon's jailed opposition leader requested France's intervention (Jul 20), amid the country's post-2023-coup political transition and continued restrictions on dissent.

top_entities.name: Pakistan
top_entities.news_summary: Operation Shaaban militant-death toll reached 91 by Jul 16 (129 combined with intelligence-based operations since Jul 5), continuing near-daily reports for a second week, following the Jul 4-8 coordinated TTP/BLA assaults that killed 42.

top_entities.name: Delhi
top_entities.news_summary: More than 10,000 "Cockroach" movement protesters faced tear gas and baton charges in New Delhi (Jul 20) demanding the Education Minister's resignation over the NEET paper-leak/CBSE marking scandal -- the largest challenge yet to the government on this issue.

top_entities.name: Ibadan
top_entities.news_summary: Alleged Yoruba-Hausa clash in the Ojoo area of Ibadan (Jul 8) left roads blocked and properties set ablaze; separate tension followed the killing of a tricycle mechanic.

top_entities.name: Tucson
top_entities.news_summary: Mass shooting in downtown Tucson (Jul 19) left 10 people wounded including the suspect; officers on foot patrol engaged the shooter after gunfire erupted in a busy commercial district.

top_entities.name: Verizon
top_entities.news_summary: Verizon announced (Jul 16) it will sell 274 company-owned retail stores and cut ~500 corporate jobs (~3,000 employees affected), bringing cumulative headcount reductions since Oct 2025 to 16,600+.

top_entities.name: Armenia
top_entities.news_summary: Outbreak of border violence between Armenia and Azerbaijan (Jul 12-16), including exchanges of heavy-weapon fire and drone use.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C
top_entities.news_summary: DRC Ebola (Bundibugyo strain) death toll reached 930 (2,344 confirmed cases) as of data through Jul 18, up from 600 (Jul 9) and 500 (Jul 6) — the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak on record, with dozens of health workers among the dead. Ituri province worst-hit (776 deaths).

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: US CENTCOM conducted a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran (reported Jul 18); Iran's Health Ministry says at least 50 people have been killed and 500+ injured in US strikes this month, up from an earlier 30-killed tally. Strikes have hit desalination infrastructure, depriving 20 villages of water.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: Israeli strike on a funeral in Gaza (Jul 17) killed at least 7 and wounded 22; follows Jul 16 strikes killing 5 and a "dozen killed in two days" reported Jul 15. Cumulative post-ceasefire toll: 1,123 killed / 3,616 injured since Oct 2025; overall war toll 73,098+ killed.

top_entities.name: Palestine
top_entities.news_summary: OCHA sitrep: 40 Palestinians killed and 127 injured between Jul 8-15; over 9,000 chickenpox cases across 130 health facilities in two weeks; UN condemned the targeted killing of 6 journalists in early July. Over 3,200 West Bank Palestinians displaced by settler violence in 2026.

top_entities.name: Russia
top_entities.news_summary: As the party conducting the strikes: Russian glide-bomb attack on Sumy (Jul 15) killed 5 and injured 30 in a crowded civilian area; Jul 16 Kyiv missile strike killed 2. UN OHCHR verified 65,044 civilian casualties in Ukraine since the invasion, with a sharp escalation in H1 2026.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: European Parliament voted 476-28 to call for RSF terrorist designation over the siege of El Obeid (~500,000 trapped, including 105,000 displaced); city without electricity for nearly a month, bombed water infrastructure, growing famine fears. UN OHCHR warns of impending "catastrophe."

sector_alerts.title: AI Safety Index Summer 2026: no lab scores above C+; xAI, DeepSeek, Mistral fail outright
sector_alerts.scope: ai-labs/anthropic, ai-labs/openai, ai-labs/deepmind-google, ai-labs/xai-grok, ai-labs/deepseek, ai-labs/mistral-ai
sector_alerts.summary: Future of Life Institute's Summer 2026 AI Safety Index (published Jul 7) graded 9 frontier labs across 37 indicators; Anthropic led at a C+ (2.66), OpenAI and Google DeepMind scored C, and xAI, DeepSeek and Mistral scored F. The report finds top performers have quietly walked back earlier safety pledges under competitive pressure.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act enforcement activates; chatbot disclosure rules binding, GPAI fines from Aug 2
sector_alerts.summary: EU AI Act enforcement began Jul 10, 2026, with chatbot-disclosure obligations now binding; the Digital Omnibus package (final approval Jul 9) confirmed the enforcement timeline. From Aug 2 the European Commission can fine general-purpose AI providers (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama) retroactively for violations dating to Aug 2025.

sector_alerts.title: ICE custody and enforcement deaths continue climbing; fatal shooting in Maine
sector_alerts.summary: At least 22 people have died in ICE custody in 2026 as of Jul 15; a Jul 13 death at Irwin County Detention Center (Georgia) and a Jul 14 fatal ICE shooting of a Colombian national in Biddeford, Maine, add to a mounting toll amid expanding detention capacity and record enforcement operations. Mexico has separately raised concerns over 17 Mexican nationals' deaths tied to ICE operations.

sector_alerts.title: Hunger Hotspots: famine risk sustained/deepening in Sudan, South Sudan, Gaza, Somalia
sector_alerts.scope: countries/sudan, countries/south-sudan, countries/palestine, countries/somalia, countries/djibouti, countries/nigeria
sector_alerts.summary: FAO-WFP Hunger Hotspots analysis flags 13 countries/territories at significant worsening-hunger risk through Nov 2026; active famine risk persists in Sudan, South Sudan, Gaza and Somalia. Djibouti (Jul 17) and Nigeria (Jul 16) both saw fresh confirmatory hunger-spike reporting this cycle.

sector_alerts.title: Azerbaijan-Armenia border violence flares (Jul 12-16)
sector_alerts.summary: Renewed border violence between Azerbaijan and Armenia from Jul 12-16 included exchanges of heavy-weapon fire and drone use, alongside continued unresolved right-of-return issues for ethnic Armenians displaced from Nagorno-Karabakh.

sector_alerts.title: Mexico press-freedom crisis: sixth journalist murdered in 2026
sector_alerts.summary: RSF condemned the Jul 16 killing of Josue Martinez Contreras, an online-news director, in Puebla state -- the sixth journalist murdered in Mexico in 2026 and the third in a month, despite a global 54% YoY decline in journalist killings in H1 2026.

sector_alerts.title: Global tech layoffs on pace to exceed 2025 total; ~154,000 cut in H1 2026
sector_alerts.summary: Global technology-sector layoffs reached ~154,000 in H1 2026 (already exceeding half of 2025's full-year 246,000), with the US accounting for 121,072 of the cuts; AI-driven restructuring is the leading cited cause. Not attributable to any single rotation-tracked entity without company-specific confirmation.

sector_alerts.title: China: fatal Shanxi coal-mine gas explosion drives sharpest coal-output drop in a decade
sector_alerts.summary: A gas explosion at Tongzhou Group's Liushenyu coal mine (Shanxi) killed 82+ miners; Beijing detained mine managers, suspended all four Tongzhou operations and ordered a "rigorous" investigation, driving a 9.7% YoY drop in June coal output -- the steepest decline since 2016. Underlying blast date predates this window; reported as sustaining regulatory-response context.

sector_alerts.title: Cuba: femicide count and blackout crisis both climbing
sector_alerts.summary: Cuba recorded 7 femicides in July alone (44 total in 2026, a 112% YoY increase per Alas Tensas Gender Observatory), compounding the Jul 14 third nationwide blackout in nine days amid the ongoing US fuel blockade and a record 1,306 political prisoners (Jul 9).

sector_alerts.title: India: "Cockroach" youth movement protests intensify in New Delhi
sector_alerts.summary: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party movement escalated protests at Jantar Mantar and marched on Parliament (Jul 20), met with tear gas and baton charges, over the NEET exam paper-leak and CBSE marking-irregularity scandal and demands for the Education Minister's resignation.
